name: Council of Europe Convention on Contact concerning Children 2003 (CETS 192)
summary: The Council of Europe Convention on Contact concerning Children 2003 (formally Convention on Contact concerning Children CETS 192) is a European supranational meta-framework parental-contact-rights instrument adopted by the Council of Europe — structurally distinctive globally as the only modern international binding-treaty framework operating specifically on parental + family contact rights with children (Council of Europe Convention on Contact concerning Children adopted 15 May 2003 + effective 1 September 2005 — formal European regional binding-treaty framework establishing minimum-standard parental + family contact rights with children, distinct from rest-of-modern-international-children's-rights frameworks which operate on broader best-interests-of-the-child + general-children's-rights framework, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern international binding-treaty framework operating on formal-parental-and-family-contact-rights-specific framework), as the only modern international binding-treaty framework with formal contact-rights enforcement framework (Convention Article 14 trans-frontier contact framework + Article 15 trans-frontier contact safeguards framework + Article 17 cooperation between central authorities framework — formal contact-rights enforcement framework, distinct from Hague Convention 1980 + UNCRC + ACRWC + Inter-American Convention 1989 which operate broader frameworks), as the central jurisdiction of the formal Council of Europe post-Hague-1980 parental-contact-rights elaboration framework (Council of Europe Convention 2003 adopted as European regional supplement to Hague Convention 1980 framework — Article 22 framework providing for application alongside Hague Convention 1980 + applicable to 15 of 46 Council of Europe member states that have ratified the Convention as of 2026, with substantial remaining-states-not-ratified framework reflecting limited adoption of parental-contact-rights-specific framework), and as the central jurisdiction of one of the most distinctive modern parental + family contact rights frameworks globally (Convention Article 2 contact-definition framework + Article 3 best-interests-of-the-child framework + Article 4 contact-rights of the child framework + Article 5 contact-rights of parents framework + Article 6 contact-rights of family-members framework — formal three-tier contact-rights framework, distinct from rest-of-modern-international-children's-rights frameworks operating on broader best-interests + parental-rights frameworks without formal three-tier contact-rights framework). The Convention is binding upon 15 Council of Europe member states that have ratified the Convention as of 2026: Albania + Andorra + Bosnia and Herzegovina + Croatia + Cyprus + Czech Republic + Iceland + Italy + Malta + Republic of Moldova + Romania + San Marino + Slovenia + Turkey + Ukraine. The Convention is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label, though formal contact-rights framework is highly relevant. The European Court of Human Rights is the apex judicial body for state-party violations of related rights frameworks. The Council of Europe Convention 2003 is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label.

statutory_framework.citation: Council of Europe Convention on Contact concerning Children 2003 (CETS 192)
statutory_framework.title: Council of Europe Convention on Contact concerning Children
statutory_framework.relevance: Council of Europe Convention on Contact concerning Children CETS 192 adopted 15 May 2003 + effective 1 September 2005 — formal European regional binding-treaty framework establishing minimum-standard parental + family contact rights with children.

statutory_framework.title: Hague Convention 1980
statutory_framework.relevance: Hague Convention on the Civil Aspects of International Child Abduction 1980 — concurrent applicability framework with Council of Europe Convention 2003 via Article 22 framework.

statutory_framework.citation: Council of Europe Convention on the Exercise of Children's Rights 1996 (CETS 160)
statutory_framework.title: Council of Europe Convention on the Exercise of Children's Rights
statutory_framework.relevance: Council of Europe Convention on the Exercise of Children's Rights CETS 160 adopted 25 January 1996 + effective 1 July 2000 — formal European regional children's-rights binding-treaty framework, complementary framework with Council of Europe Convention 2003.

statutory_framework.citation: European Convention on Human Rights 1950 Article 8 (Right to Respect for Family Life)
statutory_framework.title: European Convention on Human Rights Article 8
statutory_framework.relevance: European Convention on Human Rights ECHR Article 8 Right to Respect for Family Life — formal European regional human-rights framework underlying Council of Europe Convention 2003 contact-rights framework.

apex_courts.name: European Court of Human Rights (ECtHR)
apex_courts.role: Apex judicial body for state-party violations of related rights frameworks including ECHR Article 8 Right to Respect for Family Life applicable to contact-rights framework.

apex_courts.name: Council of Europe Standing Committee T-CC
apex_courts.role: Council of Europe Standing Committee T-CC — formal monitoring framework for Council of Europe Convention on Contact concerning Children 2003.

professional_regulators.name: Council of Europe Secretariat
professional_regulators.role: Council of Europe Convention on Contact concerning Children 2003 implementation framework.

anonymisation_convention: Council of Europe Convention 2003-related individual communications are anonymised per Council of Europe + ECtHR practice using initials.

key_developments.title: Council of Europe founded
key_developments.description: Council of Europe founded 5 May 1949 by Statute of the Council of Europe — beginning of formal Council of Europe regional framework.

key_developments.title: ECHR adopted + Article 8
key_developments.description: European Convention on Human Rights ECHR adopted 4 November 1950 + effective 3 September 1953 — formal ECHR framework including Article 8 Right to Respect for Family Life applicable to contact-rights framework.

key_developments.title: Hague Convention 1980 adopted
key_developments.description: Hague Convention on the Civil Aspects of International Child Abduction adopted 25 October 1980 — universal multilateral framework supplementary to Council of Europe Convention 2003.

key_developments.title: Council of Europe Convention on the Exercise of Children's Rights
key_developments.description: Council of Europe Convention on the Exercise of Children's Rights CETS 160 adopted 25 January 1996 — formal European regional children's-rights binding-treaty framework.

key_developments.title: Council of Europe Convention 2003 adopted
key_developments.description: Council of Europe Convention on Contact concerning Children CETS 192 adopted 15 May 2003 — formal European regional parental-contact-rights binding-treaty framework.

key_developments.title: Council of Europe Convention 2003 enters force
key_developments.description: Council of Europe Convention on Contact concerning Children CETS 192 entered force 1 September 2005 — formal European regional parental-contact-rights binding-treaty framework operational.

structural_findings: Council of Europe Convention 2003 operates a supranational European regional parental-contact-rights binding-treaty framework — places Council of Europe Convention 2003 in the European supranational-meta-framework cluster.

Only modern international binding-treaty framework operating specifically on parental + family contact rights with children is structurally distinctive globally — formal-parental-and-family-contact-rights-specific framework.
Only modern international binding-treaty framework with formal contact-rights enforcement framework is structurally distinctive globally — Convention Article 14 + 15 + 17 frameworks.

Central jurisdiction of formal Council of Europe post-Hague-1980 parental-contact-rights elaboration framework is structurally distinctive globally — Article 22 concurrent applicability framework with Hague Convention 1980.

Central jurisdiction of one of most distinctive modern parental + family contact rights frameworks globally is structurally distinctive globally — Convention Article 2 + 3 + 4 + 5 + 6 frameworks + formal three-tier contact-rights framework.

15 of 46 Council of Europe member state party ratification framework is structurally distinctive globally — relatively-limited European state party ratification framework reflecting limited adoption of parental-contact-rights-specific framework.

1949 Council of Europe + 1950 ECHR + 1980 Hague Convention + 1996 Council of Europe Convention on the Exercise of Children's Rights + 2003 Council of Europe Convention regional-framework-development sequence is structurally distinctive globally.
Concurrent applicability framework with Hague Convention 1980 via Article 22 framework is structurally distinctive globally.
editorial_notes: Council of Europe Convention on Contact concerning Children 2003 jurisdiction sidecar — supranational European regional parental-contact-rights binding-treaty meta-framework (Council of Europe Convention 2003/2005 + Hague Convention 1980/1983 + Council of Europe Convention on the Exercise of Children's Rights 1996/2000 + ECHR 1950/1953 Article 8 + Council of Europe founded 1949 + ECtHR). Only modern international binding-treaty framework operating specifically on parental + family contact rights with children globally + only modern international binding-treaty framework with formal contact-rights enforcement framework + central jurisdiction of formal Council of Europe post-Hague-1980 parental-contact-rights elaboration framework + central jurisdiction of one of most distinctive modern parental + family contact rights frameworks globally + 15 of 46 Council of Europe member state party ratification framework + 1949 Council of Europe + 1950 ECHR + 1980 Hague Convention + 1996 Council of Europe Convention on the Exercise of Children's Rights + 2003 Council of Europe Convention regional-framework-development sequence + Concurrent applicability framework with Hague Convention 1980 via Article 22 framework.
PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator.

Joins European + supranational + regional-parental-contact-rights-binding-treaty cluster + Council-of-Europe-Convention-2003-only-international-binding-treaty-framework-operating-on-parental-and-family-contact-rights-globally-distinctive + Article-14-trans-frontier-contact-framework + Article-22-concurrent-applicability-with-Hague-Convention-1980 + three-tier-contact-rights-framework-Article-4-5-6 + 15-of-46-Council-of-Europe-state-party-ratification + 1949-Council-of-Europe + ECHR-Article-8-Right-to-Respect-for-Family-Life + Council-of-Europe-Convention-on-the-Exercise-of-Children's-Rights-1996 clusters within the corpus.
